specialism.name: docs-index
specialism.task: Write or improve the documentation site landing page (typically docs/src/index.md). This page is the conceptual entry point for readers already inside the documentation site. Its job is to orient newcomers, describe what the package is and why it exists, and route them to the right topic. It uses Documenter.jl cross-reference syntax throughout (@ref links, optional @raw html for embedded visuals) and must not duplicate content that belongs on other pages (installation belongs to the installation page; worked examples belong to tutorials). The output is a landing page that a reader can scan in under a minute and immediately know where to go next.

workflow.step: Orient yourself. Read the project's README.md (if it exists) to understand the package pitch and any "Quick links" section that may need mirroring. Note the deployed docs URL — it is used by README.md but must be translated to @ref form on this page. Also read docs/make.jl to understand the sidebar order and which pages exist (these are the navigation targets for cross-references in step 3).

Locate the index page: find docs/src -maxdepth 1 -name 'index.md' Read it and identify: missing or unclear package description, missing cross-references to key pages, installation instructions that should be on their own page, code demos that belong in a tutorial, badges (which belong in the README, not here), or absolute doc URLs that should be @ref links.

Draft or edit the page following the structure below. Keep the index page conceptual — it describes and routes; it does not teach or install.
Canonical structure: 1. H1 = package name (display name, may omit .jl suffix). 2. One descriptive paragraph: what the package is, who it is for, and what distinguishes it. Third-person, no jargon without a brief gloss. 3. (Optional) Feature or algorithm catalog with H2 sub-sections — use @raw html blocks to embed animated GIFs or figures if project assets exist under docs/src/assets/. 4. (Optional) H2 "Quick links" — if the README has this section, mirror it here with absolute URLs translated to @ref form. If the README does not have this section, omit it on this page too. 5. (Optional) H2 "Authors" or "Citation" — short attribution block.
Omit: badges, Pkg.add install instructions, runnable code demos, API listings.
Verify all @ref links resolve. Run: julia --project=docs docs/make.jl Resolve any Documenter warnings introduced by the edits.

rubric.criterion: All internal links use Documenter.jl @ref form: [`Symbol`](@ref), [label](@ref Symbol), or [label](@ref anchor). No absolute doc URLs (those belong in the README). No relative Markdown paths (use @ref, not ../../api.md).
The index page does not contain Pkg.add instructions, clone steps, or test commands. Those belong on the installation page. A single sentence pointing to the installation page via @ref is sufficient.
Badge images and CI status widgets belong in the README, not on the docs landing page.
A reader unfamiliar with the package can understand what it does and why it exists within the first paragraph. Technical terms are either explained or linked.
If README.md has a "Quick links" section, this page mirrors it with absolute URLs translated to @ref form. Content stays in sync; link form is the only difference.
The docs build produces no more Documenter warnings after the edit than before.

formatting.rule: H1 = package display name; sentence-case for all sub-headings.
Third-person descriptive voice throughout ("MyPackage is a library...", "it provides...", "the package implements...").
Cross-references to documented symbols: [`MyType`](@ref) or [label](@ref MyType).
Cross-references to pages or anchors: [label](@ref anchor-name).
Embedded visuals (if used): @raw html blocks for size-controlled images or GIFs, not plain Markdown ![alt](path) — Documenter renders both, but @raw html allows width control without CSS.
No code fences unless showing brief illustrative output (not a full runnable demo).
No @raw html for layout tricks; only for embedded media.
No badges, no Pkg.add snippets, no clone commands.
formatting.example:
## Before (badges present, install instructions, absolute doc URL)
# MyPackage
[![CI](https://github.com/CliMA/MyPackage.jl/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](...)
Install: `using Pkg; Pkg.add("MyPackage")`
MyPackage solves inverse problems. See the [API](https://clima.github.io/MyPackage.jl/dev/api/).
## After (no badges, no install, @ref links, third-person pitch)
# MyPackage
MyPackage is a Julia library for estimating unknown parameters from noisy observations using ensemble-based inversion algorithms. It is designed for scientific computing workflows that require derivative-free, parallelisable calibration at scale.
## Quick links
- [Installation](@ref installation) - [Getting started](@ref getting-started) - [API reference](@ref api)
